name: Persian Beef Koobideh
ingredients:
1 kg ground beef (70/30 lean/fat)
1 large brown onion
1 large egg
1 tbsp kosher salt
1/2 tsp ground turmeric
1/2 tsp ground black pepper
1 tbsp saffron water
directions:
1. Grate the onion finely. Squeeze out and discard the excess liquid - too much moisture and the meat slips off the skewers.
2. In a bowl, combine the squeezed onion with salt, turmeric, black pepper, saffron water, and egg. Whisk to combine.
3. Add the beef. Knead with your hands for 2-3 minutes until uniform and slightly tacky - this protein bind is what holds the meat on the skewer.
4. Cover and refrigerate at least 30 minutes (up to 2 hours for deeper flavor).
5. Preheat grill to high. Keep a bowl of warm water beside you.
6. Wet hands, take ~100 g of meat, shape into a cylinder around a flat Persian skewer. Press firmly along the skewer, re-wet hands, smooth the surface, and pinch ridges with two fingers along its length. Trim excess at the tips.
7. Place on the hot grill. Flip every 30 seconds for the first 1-2 minutes to seal the meat to the skewer.
8. Continue grilling, turning every 1-2 minutes, until cooked through and lightly charred - 6-8 minutes total. Meat should feel firm.
9. To remove: press down from the top of the skewer, slide off from the bottom.
10. Serve immediately with chelo, grilled tomato, sumac, and fresh herbs.
notes:
Saffron water: grind 1/4 tsp saffron threads with a pinch of salt in a mortar. Steep in 3 tbsp hot (not boiling) water for 10+ min. Keeps refrigerated 1 week.

70/30 fat ratio is non-negotiable - leaner meat falls off the skewers.

Flat Persian skewers (~1 inch wide) are required; round skewers won't grip the meat.
